title: Stripe PaymentIntent creation and webhook order confirmation
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Two endpoints that together form the complete checkout flow:

1. POST /payments/payment-intent — authenticated Buyer passes their cartId, BE creates a Stripe PaymentIntent for the cart total in USD, returns the clientSecret to the frontend.

2. POST /payments/webhook — receives Stripe payment_intent.succeeded events. Verifies the webhook signature (mandatory — unsigned requests are rejected with 400). On success: creates an Order record, forwards line items to MockSupplierService, sends an order confirmation email via Resend, and deletes the Cart. The entire flow is idempotent: a duplicate webhook for the same PaymentIntent is a no-op.

The webhook endpoint receives a raw Buffer (registered before the global JSON parser in main.ts) so Stripe signature verification works correctly.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/payments/payment-intent` with a valid cartId returns `{ clientSecret: "..." }`
- [ ] PaymentIntent amount equals the sum of (SKU price × quantity) across all CartItems, in cents
- [ ] `POST /api/payments/webhook` with valid signature and `payment_intent.succeeded` creates an Order with status CONFIRMED
- [ ] Order is then updated to FORWARDED with a supplierReference after MockSupplierService.submitOrder resolves
- [ ] Order confirmation email is sent via Resend after forwarding
- [ ] Cart is deleted after Order is confirmed
- [ ] Second webhook with the same PaymentIntent ID is a no-op (Order not duplicated)
- [ ] Webhook with invalid signature returns 400 (no Order created)
- [ ] Integration test: mock Stripe constructEvent, verify Order created + Cart deleted
- [ ] Integration test: second webhook with same PaymentIntentId — only one Order exists
- [ ] Unit test: invalid signature rejected before any DB writes

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-q9l
close_reason: Tests written and passing: 1 unit test (invalid signature → BadRequestException, no DB writes) + 5 integration tests (payment-intent clientSecret, amount in cents, webhook creates Order+deletes Cart, notification sent, idempotent duplicate webhook)

title: Account Cart and guest-to-account merge
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Authenticated Buyers get a persistent Cart tied to their Account.id (resolved via AccountsService.findOrCreate). Cart behavior is identical to the guest cart but keyed on accountId instead of sessionId and has no TTL. On login, the frontend calls POST /cart/merge with the guest sessionId to fold guest cart items into the account cart — duplicate SKUs keep the account cart's quantity, and the guest cart is deleted.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/cart` with a valid Clerk JWT returns (or creates) the Account's Cart
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` adds items to the Account Cart
- [ ] `DELETE /api/cart/items/:skuId` removes items from the Account Cart
- [ ] Account Cart has no expiresAt (null)
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/merge` with a valid `sessionId` body moves all guest cart items into the Account Cart
- [ ] Merging a SKU already in the Account Cart keeps the Account Cart's quantity (does not double-count)
- [ ] Guest Cart is deleted after a successful merge
- [ ] Merging a non-existent sessionId is a no-op (no error)
- [ ] Integration test: full account cart lifecycle
- [ ] Integration test: merge guest cart into account cart, verify guest cart gone and items present

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-j0g
- merch-shop-BE-pzv

title: Admin catalog write API: CRUD for catalog entities and SKU availability
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Admin-only write endpoints for the full catalog: create/update/delete Publishers, Games, Teams, Characters, Products, and SKUs. Also includes the SKU availability toggle (PATCH /skus/:id/availability) which is the primary operational control for the drop-ship model. All endpoints require AdminGuard (Clerk JWT + Account.role === ADMIN in DB).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/publishers` (AdminGuard) creates a Publisher; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] `PATCH /api/publishers/:id` updates a Publisher; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] `DELETE /api/publishers/:id` deletes a Publisher; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] Same pattern works for Games, Teams, Characters, Products
- [ ] `POST /api/skus` creates a SKU under a Product with price and attributes
- [ ] `PATCH /api/skus/:id/availability` flips the SKU's available boolean; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] AdminGuard returns 401 when no Bearer token is present
- [ ] AdminGuard returns 403 when token is valid but Account.role is BUYER
- [ ] Integration test: Admin creates Publisher → Game → Team → Product → SKU end-to-end
- [ ] Integration test: BUYER role rejected on all write endpoints

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-giv
- merch-shop-BE-j0g
close_reason: All admin CRUD endpoints verified via integration tests. 44 admin tests pass (positive write + 403 guard coverage for all 6 entities + end-to-end chain).

title: Guest Cart: UUID-cookie cart with availability check
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

A server-side Cart for Guests, identified by a UUID stored in an HttpOnly cookie. Guests can view their Cart, add SKUs, update quantities, and remove items — all without authenticating. The Cart persists in PostgreSQL with accountId=null and an expiresAt TTL. Adding an unavailable SKU is rejected with 400.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/cart` with no auth and no cookie creates a new guest Cart and sets a `cart_session` HttpOnly cookie
- [ ] `GET /api/cart` with an existing `cart_session` cookie returns the same Cart
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` with a valid available SKU adds the item (or updates quantity if already present)
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` with an unavailable SKU returns 400
- [ ] `POST /api/cart/items` with a non-existent SKU returns 404
- [ ] `DELETE /api/cart/items/:skuId` removes the item from the Cart
- [ ] Cart rows with accountId=null have an expiresAt set to GUEST_CART_TTL_DAYS days from creation
- [ ] Integration tests: full guest cart lifecycle (create → add → update quantity → remove)
- [ ] Integration test: unavailable SKU rejected before Cart is modified

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-81u

title: Account provisioning: upsert Account on first auth
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Two things in one vertical slice:

**1. AccountModule + AccountService seam (arch prefactor)**
Account is a first-class domain concept with no module. AdminGuard performs a raw Prisma query to check account role — bypassing any seam. Create AccountModule with AccountService exposing: `hasRole(accountId, role)`, `upsertFromClerk(clerkUser)`, `findById(accountId)`. AdminGuard calls `AccountService.hasRole()` — no direct Prisma in the guard.

**2. Account provisioning on first auth (feature)**
On the first request with a valid Clerk JWT, upsert an Account record linked to the Clerk user ID. Subsequent requests return the existing Account. A `GET /api/account/me` endpoint returns the current Account.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] AccountModule exports AccountService
- [ ] AccountService.upsertFromClerk() creates or updates Account on Clerk JWT validation
- [ ] AccountService.hasRole(id, role) used by AdminGuard — no direct Prisma in guard
- [ ] `GET /api/account/me` returns current Account (401 if unauthenticated)
- [ ] Integration test: first request with valid Clerk JWT creates Account; second request returns same Account
- [ ] App compiles and existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

merch-shop-BE-772 — Prefactor: surface PrismaModule coupling in module declarations
close_reason: AccountModule + AccountService + GET /api/account/me implemented. AdminGuard refactored to use AccountService.hasRole. All 26 integration tests pass.

title: Catalog read API: public browse + facet filtering
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Public GET endpoints that let Buyers browse the full catalog and filter Products by orthogonal facets. No authentication required. All six catalog resources (Publisher, Game, Team, Character, Product, SKU) are readable. Products support combined facet filtering via query params — any combination of gameId, teamId, and characterId is valid.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/publishers` returns all Publishers ordered by name
- [ ] `GET /api/publishers/:slug` returns a single Publisher or 404
- [ ] `GET /api/games` returns all Games; `?publisherId=` filters by Publisher
- [ ] `GET /api/games/:slug` returns a single Game with its Publisher
- [ ] `GET /api/teams` returns all Teams; `?gameId=` filters by Game
- [ ] `GET /api/characters` returns all Characters; `?gameId=` filters by Game
- [ ] `GET /api/products` returns all Products with their available SKUs and facet relations
- [ ] `GET /api/products?gameId=X` returns only Products with that Game facet
- [ ] `GET /api/products?gameId=X&teamId=Y` returns Products matching both facets
- [ ] `GET /api/products/:id` returns a single Product with full SKU list and facet relations
- [ ] `GET /api/skus?productId=X` returns all SKUs for a Product
- [ ] Integration tests cover ProductsService.findAll with no filters, single facet, and combined facets
- [ ] Integration tests confirm unavailable SKUs are excluded from the findAll SKU list

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-81u

title: Project bootstrap: install, migrate, start
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Get the project running end-to-end locally so every subsequent slice has a working baseline. This means: dependencies installed, Postgres running via Docker Compose, Prisma migrations applied, app starts without errors, and Swagger UI is reachable.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `npm install` completes without errors
- [ ] `.env` created from `.env.example` with valid local values (Postgres credentials, stub keys for Clerk/Stripe/Resend)
- [ ] `docker compose up db` starts Postgres on port 5432
- [ ] `npx prisma migrate dev --name init` applies the full schema with no errors
- [ ] `npm run start:dev` starts the app without runtime errors
- [ ] `GET /api/docs` returns 200 and Swagger UI renders all routes
- [ ] `GET /api/publishers` returns an empty array (not a 500)

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.
close_reason: App starts, migrations applied, GET /api/publishers → 200 [], GET /api/docs → 200. Two TS type fixes in skus.service and payments.service.

title: E-Sport Merch Shop Backend — Full MVP Build
description:
## Problem Statement

Buyers wanting to purchase licensed e-sport merchandise have no backend to serve catalog data, manage their cart, or process orders. Without a backend, the storefront cannot display products filtered by Game, Team, or Character facets; cannot persist a Buyer's Cart across sessions; and cannot accept payment or forward confirmed Orders to a Supplier for fulfillment.

## Solution

A NestJS REST API backed by PostgreSQL (via Prisma) that:
- Serves a multi-publisher product catalog filterable by orthogonal facets (Game, Team, Character)
- Persists Carts server-side for both Guests (UUID cookie) and authenticated Accounts (Clerk JWT)
- Accepts Stripe payments via a webhook-driven flow that confirms Orders and forwards them to a Supplier
- Sends order confirmation emails via Resend
- Exposes an Admin API (role-gated) for catalog and SKU management

## User Stories

1. As a Buyer, I want to browse all Products so that I can discover merch across all publishers and games.
2. As a Buyer, I want to filter Products by Game so that I can see only merch for a specific game I follow.
3. As a Buyer, I want to filter Products by Team so that I can find merch for my favourite esport team.
4. As a Buyer, I want to filter Products by Character so that I can browse merch for a specific in-game figure.
5. As a Buyer, I want to combine facet filters (e.g. Game + Team) so that I can narrow results to exactly what I want.
6. As a Buyer, I want to view a Product's detail page including all available SKUs so that I can choose the right size, colour, or variant.
7. As a Guest, I want to add a SKU to my Cart without logging in so that I can shop without creating an Account.
8. As a Guest, I want my Cart to persist across page reloads and tab closes so that I do not lose my selections.
9. As a Guest, I want to update quantities or remove items from my Cart so that I can adjust my purchase before checkout.
10. As a Guest, I want to complete checkout and place an Order with just my email so that I do not need an Account to buy.
11. As an Account holder, I want my Cart to persist across devices so that I can start shopping on one device and finish on another.
12. As an Account holder, I want my Guest Cart to merge into my Account Cart when I log in so that I do not lose items I added before signing in.
13. As an Account holder, I want to initiate payment for my Cart and receive a Stripe client secret so that I can complete payment securely on the frontend.
14. As a Buyer, I want to receive an order confirmation email after successful payment so that I have a record of my purchase.
15. As a Buyer, I want to view my Order history so that I can track past purchases.
16. As an Admin, I want to create and update Publishers so that I can onboard new game companies to the platform.
17. As an Admin, I want to create and update Games under a Publisher so that I can expand the catalog to new esport titles.
18. As an Admin, I want to create and update Teams under a Game so that I can manage competing organisations on the platform.
19. As an Admin, I want to create and update Characters under a Game so that I can add in-game figures as product facets.
20. As an Admin, I want to create Products and assign them any combination of Game, Team, and Character facets so that I can accurately tag merch that crosses multiple dimensions.
21. As an Admin, I want to create SKUs under a Product with a price and attributes (size, colour, etc.) so that I can offer variants at correct prices.
22. As an Admin, I want to flip a SKU's Availability flag so that I can remove unavailable variants from the Buyer's cart and catalog without deleting them.
23. As an Admin, I want to view all Orders so that I can monitor purchases and handle support queries.
24. As the system, I want to verify Stripe webhook signatures so that I only process legitimate payment events.
25. As the system, I want to create an Order idempotently on payment_intent.succeeded so that retried webhooks do not produce duplicate Orders.
26. As the system, I want to forward a confirmed Order to the Supplier so that physical goods are dispatched to the Buyer.
27. As the system, I want to delete the Cart after an Order is confirmed so that stale cart data does not accumulate.
28. As the system, I want to rate-limit checkout and auth endpoints so that abuse and card-testing attacks are mitigated.

## Implementation Decisions

- **Framework**: NestJS with TypeScript. Modules map directly to domain boundaries: Catalog, Commerce, Fulfillment, Payments, Notifications, Auth.

- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM. Schema uses a Publisher → Game → Team/Character hierarchy for catalog entities. Products carry nullable foreign keys to Game, Team, and Character — any combination is valid (orthogonal facets, per ADR-0002).

- **Auth**: Authentication is fully delegated to Clerk (per ADR-0004). The BE exposes no login or registration endpoints. A ClerkGuard verifies the Bearer JWT on protected routes using Clerk's JWKS. An AdminGuard extends ClerkGuard with a DB lookup to confirm the Account has role ADMIN.

- **Guest Cart**: Guest Carts are stored in PostgreSQL with accountId=null, identified by a UUID in an HttpOnly cookie (per ADR-0006). Cart rows with null accountId carry an expiresAt TTL. On Account login, the frontend calls POST /cart/merge with the guest sessionId to merge guest items into the account Cart.

- **Payments**: Stripe is the payment gateway (per ADR-0005). The frontend creates a PaymentIntent via POST /payments/payment-intent (auth required). The order confirmation flow is entirely webhook-driven: payment_intent.succeeded → confirm Order → forward to Supplier → send email → delete Cart. Webhook signature verification is mandatory. The flow is idempotent: duplicate webhooks for the same PaymentIntent are no-ops.

- **Supplier**: All fulfillment is routed through a SupplierPort interface (submitOrder, checkAvailability). The concrete implementation for MVP is MockSupplierService which logs and returns a synthetic reference. Swapping to a real Supplier requires implementing the port — no other code changes.

- **Notifications**: Resend sends a single transactional email (order confirmation) per Order. The email is triggered inside the Stripe webhook handler after Supplier forwarding succeeds.

- **Pricing**: All prices stored and transacted in USD as a Decimal(10,2). No per-currency columns exist (per ADR-0008). Stripe handles local currency display on the FE.

- **Security**: Helmet applied globally. ThrottlerModule configured with three rate-limit tiers: default (100 req/min), auth (10 req/min), checkout (5 req/min). CORS locked to FRONTEND_URL env var. Stripe webhook endpoint receives raw body before the global JSON parser.

- **Admin surface**: Admin endpoints (POST/PATCH/DELETE on catalog, GET /orders) are gated by AdminGuard. No admin UI is built; Admin interacts via REST API directly or through a future admin panel.

- **Deployment**: Docker Compose for local dev (app + postgres services). docker-compose.test.yml provides an ephemeral test DB on port 5433 using tmpfs (fast, no persistent volume).

## Testing Decisions

- **What makes a good test**: Test the service layer directly — not HTTP handlers, not Prisma internals. Assert on the return value and on follow-up DB state, not on which Prisma methods were called.

- **Unit tests** (Jest, `*.spec.ts`): Service methods with Prisma mocked via jest.fn(). Cover business rules: unavailable SKU rejected in addItem, idempotency check in confirmOrder, AdminGuard rejects non-ADMIN roles.

- **Integration tests** (Jest, `*.integration.spec.ts`, `jest.integration.config.js`): Services injected with a real PrismaService pointed at the test DB (PostgreSQL on port 5433, migrated via globalSetup). Cover: full cart lifecycle (create → add → remove → merge), full order confirmation flow (PaymentIntent → webhook → Order created + Supplier called + Cart deleted).

- **Supplier port in tests**: Integration tests inject MockSupplierService for the SUPPLIER_PORT token. No real Supplier API calls in any test environment.

- **No E2E (HTTP) tests for MVP**: The service layer is the highest shared seam. Adding HTTP-layer tests would duplicate coverage without catching new failure modes given thin controllers.

## Out of Scope

- Real Supplier integration (Printful or other) — MockSupplierService only until a Supplier contract exists
- Inventory management, stock counts, low-stock warnings, pre-order queues — Availability is a boolean flag only (per ADR-0003)
- Multi-currency pricing — USD only for MVP (per ADR-0008)
- Order cancellation, returns, or refund flows
- Admin UI — API endpoints only; UI is a separate project
- Supplier-facing portal or webhook receiver
- Search (full-text or fuzzy) — facet filtering via query params only
- Product reviews or ratings
- Discount codes, promotions, or vouchers
- Saved shipping addresses on Account

## Further Notes

- The Stripe webhook handler must be registered BEFORE the global JSON body parser in main.ts so that express.raw() receives the raw Buffer needed for signature verification.
- Guest Cart TTL is configured via GUEST_CART_TTL_DAYS env var (default 7). A periodic cleanup job to prune expired carts is out of scope for MVP but should be added before production launch.
- The MockSupplierService logs forwarded orders at INFO level — sufficient for development tracing without a real Supplier API.
- ClerkUserId (from JWT sub claim) is used as the accountId reference throughout the system — no separate internal user ID is issued.

title: Scaffold NestJS project structure
description: Initialize the full NestJS backend project for the merch shop. Includes: package.json, Prisma schema, Docker Compose, all domain modules (catalog, commerce, fulfillment, payments, notifications), Clerk guard, Stripe webhook, Resend email port.
close_reason: Full NestJS scaffold written: 45 source files, Prisma schema, Docker Compose, all domain modules

title: Retry fulfillment action for stuck Orders
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-3wn

## What to build

Add a new admin endpoint `POST /orders/:id/retry-fulfillment` that re-forwards an Order to the Supplier. The action is only valid for Orders in PENDING or CONFIRMED status that have no `supplierReference`. If the Order is already FORWARDED (has a `supplierReference`) or CANCELLED, the endpoint returns 409. The service delegates to the existing `SupplierPort` abstraction in FulfillmentModule. Endpoint is guarded by AdminGuard.

Integration test seeds an Order in PENDING status with no supplierReference, calls retry-fulfillment, and asserts the Order transitions to FORWARDED with a supplierReference set. Also asserts 409 on a second call (already FORWARDED).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `POST /api/orders/:id/retry-fulfillment` exists and is guarded by AdminGuard
- [ ] Returns 404 if Order does not exist
- [ ] Returns 409 if Order already has a `supplierReference` (already FORWARDED)
- [ ] Returns 409 if Order status is CANCELLED
- [ ] On success: delegates to SupplierPort, sets `supplierReference`, transitions Order to FORWARDED status
- [ ] Action is idempotent-safe: a second call after FORWARDED returns 409, does not double-submit to Supplier
- [ ] Integration test covers: success path (PENDING → FORWARDED), 409 on already-FORWARDED, 404 on unknown Order

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-xj3
- merch-shop-BE-rdi

title: Order list: status filter and pagination for admin
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-3wn

## What to build

Extend the existing `GET /orders` admin endpoint with optional `?status=` filter (matching the `OrderStatus` enum: PENDING, CONFIRMED, FORWARDED, CANCELLED) and pagination via the existing `PaginationQueryDto` (page, limit). The OrdersRepository `findAll()` method is updated to accept an optional status filter and pagination args and return a `PagedResult` matching the shape already used across the codebase.

Integration test in `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts` seeds Orders with different statuses, asserts status filter returns only matching Orders, and asserts pagination meta (total, page, limit) is correct.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/orders` accepts optional `?status=PENDING|CONFIRMED|FORWARDED|CANCELLED` and returns only matching Orders
- [ ] Returns 400 for invalid status value
- [ ] Accepts `?page=` and `?limit=` and returns paginated result with `meta` matching the existing `PagedResult` shape
- [ ] Unfiltered call returns all Orders (existing behavior preserved)
- [ ] Integration test: filter by FORWARDED returns only FORWARDED orders; pagination meta is accurate

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-xj3

title: Bulk SKU availability toggle by facet
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-3wn

## What to build

Add a new admin endpoint `PATCH /skus/availability/bulk` that toggles the `available` flag on all SKUs belonging to a given facet in a single database operation. The request body carries `{ facet: 'game' | 'team' | 'character', facetId: string, available: boolean }`. The service uses Prisma `updateMany` filtering on the corresponding FK column (`gameId` via Product relation, `teamId` via Product relation, `characterId` via Product relation). Endpoint is guarded by AdminGuard.

Integration test in `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts` seeds a Game with two Products each having two SKUs, calls the bulk toggle, then asserts all SKUs for that facet flipped and SKUs outside the facet were unaffected.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `PATCH /api/skus/availability/bulk` exists and is guarded by AdminGuard
- [ ] Accepts `{ facet: 'game' | 'team' | 'character', facetId: string, available: boolean }`
- [ ] Returns 400 for invalid facet value or missing facetId
- [ ] Toggles `available` on all SKUs whose Product carries the given facetId, in a single `updateMany` call (no N+1)
- [ ] SKUs outside the specified facet are unaffected
- [ ] Integration test covering: bulk disable by Game, bulk enable by Team, and no cross-facet bleed

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-xj3

title: Admin integration test harness with 403 guard coverage
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-3wn

## What to build

Create `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts` as the shared integration test harness for all admin API slices. The file bootstraps the full NestJS app (same pattern as `test/integration/catalog.integration.spec.ts`) but overrides AdminGuard to always pass, isolating business logic tests from Clerk JWT mechanics.

Also add a dedicated 403-guard describe block that boots the app WITHOUT the AdminGuard override and asserts that every admin write endpoint (Character create/update/delete, Product create/update/delete, SKU create/delete, SKU bulk availability toggle, Order retry-fulfillment) returns 403 when called without a valid admin JWT.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts` exists and bootstraps the full AppModule with AdminGuard overridden to always pass
- [ ] A `seedCatalog` / `cleanupCatalog` helper is available (can reuse or import from catalog spec) for setting up test data
- [ ] A 403-guard describe block asserts that `POST /api/characters`, `PATCH /api/characters/:id`, `DELETE /api/characters/:id`, `POST /api/products`, `PATCH /api/products/:id`, `DELETE /api/products/:id`, `POST /api/skus`, `DELETE /api/skus/:id`, `PATCH /api/skus/availability/bulk` (once built), and `POST /api/orders/:id/retry-fulfillment` (once built) all return 403 without admin role
- [ ] All existing integration tests continue to pass

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately
close_reason: Admin integration test harness created. overrideGuard() (not overrideProvider()) required for @Global() providers. 403 guard block covers all 10 admin endpoints via Prisma-only role check mock.

title: Admin Dashboard API — catalog management, order operations, bulk SKU availability
description:
## Problem Statement

Admins have no way to manage the live catalog (Characters, Products, SKUs) or act on stuck Orders through the API. The CRUD endpoints exist but are undocumented as an admin surface, bulk SKU availability toggle by facet is missing, Order listing has no status filter or pagination, and there is no retry-fulfillment action for stuck Orders.

## Solution

Expose a coherent admin API surface that covers:
- Character CRUD (live-managed catalog entity)
- Product CRUD (shell creation, then SKUs attached separately)
- SKU CRUD + bulk availability toggle by facet (Game, Team, or Character)
- Order list with status filter and pagination
- Retry fulfillment action for FORWARDED/stuck Orders

Admin identity is enforced by the existing AdminGuard (Clerk JWT + Account.role = ADMIN). Role promotion happens via Clerk dashboard only — no user-management endpoints.

## User Stories

1. As an Admin, I want to create a Character with a name, slug, image URL, and a Game selected from a dropdown, so that new in-game figures can be linked to Products.
2. As an Admin, I want to update a Character's name, slug, or image URL, so that I can correct mistakes without recreating the entity.
3. As an Admin, I want to delete a Character, so that discontinued or erroneous entries are removed from the catalog.
4. As an Admin, I want to list all Characters filterable by Game, so that I can audit which Characters belong to which Game.
5. As an Admin, I want to create a Product shell (name, description, images, and at least one facet), so that I can set up a new item before attaching SKUs.
6. As an Admin, I want to update a Product's name, description, images, or facet assignments, so that catalog metadata stays accurate.
7. As an Admin, I want to delete a Product, so that discontinued Products are removed from the storefront.
8. As an Admin, I want to create a SKU for an existing Product with a price, availability flag, and attribute map (e.g. size, colour), so that Buyers can purchase specific variants.
9. As an Admin, I want to update a SKU's price or attribute map, so that pricing and variant details stay accurate.
10. As an Admin, I want to delete a SKU, so that discontinued variants are removed.
11. As an Admin, I want to toggle a single SKU's availability on or off, so that I can quickly disable out-of-stock or discontinued variants.
12. As an Admin, I want to bulk-toggle availability for all SKUs belonging to a given facet (Game, Team, or Character), so that I can disable an entire licensed range in one action when a license expires.
13. As an Admin, I want to list all Orders with optional status filter (PENDING, CONFIRMED, FORWARDED, CANCELLED) and pagination, so that I can monitor and act on the Order lifecycle.
14. As an Admin, I want to view a single Order's full detail (items, Buyer email, shipping address, supplier reference), so that I can investigate specific Orders.
15. As an Admin, I want to retry fulfillment on an Order that failed to forward to the Supplier, so that stuck Orders do not require manual database intervention.
16. As an Admin, I want the retry-fulfillment action to be a no-op (idempotent) if the Order is already FORWARDED or CONFIRMED, so that accidental double-clicks do not cause duplicate supplier submissions.
17. As an Admin, I want all admin write endpoints to return 403 when called without a valid ADMIN-role JWT, so that catalog data is protected from unauthorized mutation.

## Implementation Decisions

- **Existing CRUD**: Character, Product, and SKU controllers already exist with AdminGuard on write operations. No new controllers needed for basic CRUD.
- **Bulk SKU availability toggle**: New endpoint `PATCH /skus/availability/bulk` accepting `{ facet: 'game' | 'team' | 'character', facetId: string, available: boolean }`. Implemented in SkusService using a Prisma `updateMany` on the facet FK column. Guarded by AdminGuard.
- **Order list enhancements**: `GET /orders` gains optional `?status=` query param and pagination via existing `PaginationQueryDto`. OrdersRepository.findAll() extended to accept filter + pagination args.
- **Retry fulfillment**: New endpoint `POST /orders/:id/retry-fulfillment` guarded by AdminGuard. OrdersService delegates to FulfillmentModule's SupplierPort. Only acts on Orders in PENDING or CONFIRMED status that have no `supplierReference`; returns 409 if already FORWARDED.
- **No schema changes**: All required columns (OrderStatus enum, SKU.available, facet FK columns) already exist in schema.prisma.
- **No new modules**: All changes land in existing Catalog and Commerce modules.
- **Admin role assignment**: Via Clerk dashboard only. No user-management endpoints in scope.
- **Publishers, Games, Teams**: Seed data only. No admin CRUD endpoints.

## Testing Decisions

- Good tests assert external HTTP behaviour (status codes, response shapes, DB side-effects) — not service internals or repository method calls.
- All tests live in a new `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts` file, matching the pattern in `test/integration/catalog.integration.spec.ts`.
- The test module bootstraps the full `AppModule` with a real PostgreSQL database (same harness as catalog tests).
- AdminGuard is overridden in the test module to always pass, so tests focus on business logic not Clerk JWT mechanics. A separate guard-level test asserts 403 is returned without admin role.
- Prior art: `test/integration/catalog.integration.spec.ts` — supertest against full NestJS app, `beforeAll`/`afterAll` seed/cleanup, `PrismaService` injected for setup.
- Modules under test: SkusService (bulk toggle), OrdersService (filter + pagination + retry-fulfillment).

## Out of Scope

- Analytics, revenue reporting, or order metrics.
- Admin UI / frontend dashboard (this PRD covers the API layer only).
- User management (promoting Accounts to ADMIN role) — done via Clerk dashboard.
- CRUD for Publishers, Games, Teams — managed via seed migrations.
- Payment cancellation or refund operations — Payments module concern.
- Supplier integration beyond retry-forwarding an existing Order.

## Further Notes

- Bulk availability toggle uses `updateMany` — no N+1 risk regardless of SKU count per facet.
- Retry fulfillment idempotency is enforced at the service layer, not the DB layer, since OrderStatus is already persisted.
- The `FORWARDED` status means the Supplier has accepted the Order; retry should only fire on Orders without a `supplierReference`.
close_reason: Bulk SKU toggle, order filter+pagination, retry-fulfillment all implemented and tested. 67 integration tests pass.

title: Implement coding conventions: common module, repository layer, domain exceptions, security, caching
description: Wire up all decisions from CONVENTIONS.md: tsconfig strict, common/ (AllExceptionsFilter, ResponseInterceptor, AuditInterceptor, BaseRepository, PaginationQueryDto), repository layer extraction for all modules, domain exceptions, barrel index.ts files, ESLint module boundary enforcement, @nestjs/event-emitter, @nestjs/cache-manager, updated app.module.ts and main.ts.
close_reason: All conventions implemented, tsc clean, 12/12 integration tests pass

title: Order history and security verification
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Two concerns completed together since both are thin and test the same running app:

**Order history**: authenticated Buyers can list their own Orders; Admins can list all Orders.

**Security verification**: confirm that Helmet security headers are present on all responses, ThrottlerModule fires 429 on burst traffic to rate-limited endpoints, and CORS rejects requests from origins not matching FRONTEND_URL.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `GET /api/orders/mine` (ClerkGuard) returns the authenticated Account's Orders with line items
- [ ] `GET /api/orders/mine` returns empty array (not 404) when Account has no Orders
- [ ] `GET /api/orders` (AdminGuard) returns all Orders; 403 for non-admin
- [ ] `GET /api/orders/:id` (ClerkGuard) returns a single Order with line items
- [ ] Integration test: Buyer can retrieve their own confirmed Order after the webhook flow
- [ ] Integration test: Admin sees all Orders; Buyer sees only their own
- [ ] Smoke test: response headers include `X-Content-Type-Options`, `X-Frame-Options` (Helmet present)
- [ ] Smoke test: 11 rapid requests to a throttled endpoint returns at least one 429
- [ ] Smoke test: request with `Origin: http://evil.example.com` is rejected by CORS (missing or blocked CORS headers)

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-87f

title: Soft delete: Account (delete endpoint + auth lockout)
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-02p

## What to build

Wire soft delete end-to-end for `Account`, including a new delete endpoint and immediate auth lockout.

`AccountRepository`: add `deletedAt: null` filter to all read queries. Add a call to `softRemove` in `AccountService.remove`.

New endpoint: `DELETE /accounts/:id` (Admin-only, guarded by `AdminGuard`). No such endpoint exists today — add it to the Account controller and service.

`ClerkGuard` change: after verifying the Clerk token and loading the `Account` row, check `account.deletedAt`. If non-null, throw `UnauthorizedException`. This ensures a soft-deleted Account is locked out on every subsequent request without any Clerk-side action.

Integration tests:
- Seed Account → DELETE /accounts/:id (as Admin) → 200
- Seed Account → DELETE → subsequent authenticated request with that Account's token → 401
- Seed Account with Orders → DELETE account → Orders remain intact (accountId set to null via FK SET NULL behavior is unchanged for hard-delete path; soft-delete leaves accountId intact on Orders, which is also acceptable since the row still exists)
- Non-Admin attempting DELETE /accounts/:id → 403

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DELETE /accounts/:id` is Admin-only and sets `deletedAt` on the Account
- [ ] Any authenticated request from a soft-deleted Account returns 401
- [ ] Orders belonging to a deleted Account remain accessible to Admins
- [ ] Non-admin `DELETE /accounts/:id` returns 403
- [ ] All existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-58m

title: Soft delete: Publisher (cascade to Games and full subtree)
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-02p

## What to build

Wire soft delete end-to-end for `Publisher`, cascading to its entire subtree via the Game cascade.

`PublishersRepository`: add `deletedAt: null` filter to all read queries (`findAll`, `findBySlug`, `findById`).

`PublishersService.remove`: call `softRemove` on the Publisher, then soft-delete all Games where `publisherId` matches — delegate to `GamesService.remove` to reuse the full Game→Teams/Characters/Products/SKUs cascade.

`DELETE /publishers/:id` now soft-deletes. No controller change needed.

`GET /publishers/:slug` and list endpoints return 404 / exclude soft-deleted Publishers.

Integration tests:
- Seed Publisher → DELETE publisher → GET publisher returns 404
- Seed Publisher + Game + Team + Products + SKUs → DELETE publisher → all return 404
- Seed Publisher + Game + Character + Products + SKUs → DELETE publisher → all return 404
- Deleted Publisher absent from list responses

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DELETE /publishers/:id` sets `deletedAt` on the Publisher
- [ ] All Games belonging to the Publisher are soft-deleted on the same request, triggering their full subtree cascade
- [ ] `GET /publishers/:slug` returns 404 for a soft-deleted Publisher
- [ ] Deleted Publishers absent from list responses
- [ ] All existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-9dc
close_reason: Publisher soft delete + cascade to Games (full subtree) implemented, 5 integration test slices passing

title: Soft delete: Game (cascade to Teams, Characters, Products, SKUs)
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-02p

## What to build

Wire soft delete end-to-end for `Game`, cascading to all entities beneath it in the hierarchy.

`GamesRepository`: add `deletedAt: null` filter to all read queries (`findAll`, `findBySlug`, `findById`).

`GamesService.remove`: call `softRemove` on the Game, then cascade:
1. Soft-delete all Teams where `gameId` matches — delegate to `TeamsService.remove` to reuse Team→Product→SKU cascade.
2. Soft-delete all Characters where `gameId` matches — delegate to `CharactersService.remove` to reuse Character→Product→SKU cascade.
3. Soft-delete all Products where `gameId` matches — delegate to `ProductsService.remove` to catch Products not already caught via Team/Character cascade (a Product with only `gameId` set and no teamId/characterId).

`DELETE /games/:id` now soft-deletes. No controller change needed.

`GET /games/:slug` and list endpoints return 404 / exclude soft-deleted Games.

Integration tests:
- Seed Game → DELETE game → GET game returns 404
- Seed Game + Team + Products + SKUs → DELETE game → all return 404
- Seed Game + Character + Products + SKUs → DELETE game → all return 404
- Seed Game + Product (gameId only, no teamId/characterId) + SKUs → DELETE game → product + SKUs return 404
- Deleted Game absent from list responses

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DELETE /games/:id` sets `deletedAt` on the Game
- [ ] All Teams, Characters, and direct Products (by gameId) are soft-deleted on the same request
- [ ] All SKUs beneath those entities are soft-deleted via their respective cascades
- [ ] `GET /games/:slug` returns 404 for a soft-deleted Game
- [ ] Deleted Games absent from list responses
- [ ] All existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-rhj
- merch-shop-BE-qw8

title: Soft delete: Character (cascade to Products and SKUs)
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-02p

## What to build

Wire soft delete end-to-end for `Character`, including cascade to Products and SKUs tagged with that Character. Parallel to the Team slice — same shape, different Facet.

`CharactersRepository`: add `deletedAt: null` filter to all read queries (`findAll`, `findBySlug`, `findById`).

`CharactersService.remove`: call `softRemove` on the Character, then cascade soft-delete all Products where `characterId` equals the deleted Character's id. Delegate each Product's cascade to `ProductsService.remove` to reuse the Product→SKU cascade.

`DELETE /characters/:id` now soft-deletes instead of hard-deletes. No controller change needed.

`GET /characters/:slug` and list endpoints return 404 / exclude soft-deleted Characters.

Integration tests:
- Seed Character → DELETE character → GET character returns 404
- Seed Character + Product (characterId set) + SKUs → DELETE character → GET product returns 404, GET SKU returns 404
- Seed Character → DELETE character → character absent from list response
- Seed Character + Product with both characterId AND gameId (active game) → DELETE character → product is still soft-deleted (any deleted parent cascades)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DELETE /characters/:id` sets `deletedAt` on the Character
- [ ] All Products with `characterId` matching the deleted Character are soft-deleted on the same request
- [ ] All SKUs belonging to those Products are soft-deleted (via Product cascade)
- [ ] `GET /characters/:slug` returns 404 for a soft-deleted Character
- [ ] Deleted Characters absent from list responses
- [ ] All existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-8di

merch-shop-BE-02p

Wire soft delete end-to-end for `Team`, including cascade to Products and SKUs tagged with that Team.

`TeamsRepository`: add `deletedAt: null` filter to all read queries (`findAll`, `findBySlug`, `findById`).

`TeamsService.remove`: call `softRemove` on the Team, then cascade soft-delete all Products where `teamId` equals the deleted Team's id. Each cascaded Product soft-delete must also cascade to its SKUs (delegate to `ProductsService.remove` to reuse the Product→SKU cascade from the Product slice).

`DELETE /teams/:id` now soft-deletes instead of hard-deletes. No controller change needed.

- Seed Team → DELETE team → team absent from list response

- [ ] All Products with `teamId` matching the deleted Team are soft-deleted on the same request

title: Soft delete: Product and SKU
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-02p

## What to build

Wire soft delete end-to-end for `Product` and `Sku` — the two leaf-level catalog entities that Admins manage directly at runtime.

`ProductsRepository` and `SkusRepository`: add `deletedAt: null` filter to every read query (`findById`, `findAll`, `findOneWithRelations`, `findByProduct`, etc.).

`ProductsService.remove`: call `softRemove` on the Product, then cascade by calling `SkusService.remove` (or `softRemove` directly) for every SKU belonging to that Product.

`SkusService.remove`: call `softRemove` on the SKU.

Both existing `DELETE /products/:id` and `DELETE /skus/:id` endpoints now soft-delete instead of hard-delete. No controller changes needed — the service layer handles it.

`GET /products/:id` and `GET /skus/:id` (and list endpoints) return 404 for soft-deleted entities, identical to a non-existent entity.

Integration tests (new file or section in existing admin/catalog integration spec):
- Seed Product + SKUs → DELETE product → GET product returns 404
- Seed Product + SKUs → DELETE product → GET each SKU returns 404 (cascade)
- Seed SKU → DELETE SKU → GET SKU returns 404
- Seed Product → DELETE product → product absent from list response
- Seed SKU via product → Order references SKU → DELETE SKU → Order detail still intact

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `DELETE /products/:id` sets `deletedAt` on the Product and all its SKUs
- [ ] `DELETE /skus/:id` sets `deletedAt` on that SKU only
- [ ] `GET /products/:id` returns 404 for a soft-deleted Product
- [ ] `GET /skus/:id` (if endpoint exists) returns 404 for a soft-deleted SKU
- [ ] Deleted Products absent from `GET /products` list
- [ ] OrderItem referencing a soft-deleted SKU remains intact (no FK breakage)
- [ ] All existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-58m

title: Soft delete foundation: schema migration + BaseRepository.softRemove
description:
## Parent

merch-shop-BE-02p

## What to build

Add the `deletedAt DateTime?` column to all seven entities that will support soft delete, and extend `BaseRepository` with a `softRemove` method. This is a pure infrastructure slice — no query filters, no behavior changes, no API differences.

Entities receiving `deletedAt`: `Publisher`, `Game`, `Team`, `Character`, `Product`, `Sku`, `Account`.

Add a single Prisma migration that alters all seven tables in one operation.

Add `softRemove(id: string)` to `BaseRepository` that sets `deletedAt` to the current timestamp via an `update` call. Keep the existing `remove(id)` hard-delete method unchanged — Cart and CartItem still depend on it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Prisma migration applies cleanly against a fresh DB with no errors
- [ ] `deletedAt` column exists (nullable timestamp, default null) on all seven tables
- [ ] `BaseRepository.softRemove(id)` sets `deletedAt = now()` and returns the updated entity
- [ ] `BaseRepository.remove(id)` still performs a hard delete (unchanged)
- [ ] All existing integration tests pass after migration

## Blocked by

None - can start immediately

title: Soft delete for catalog entities and accounts
description:
## Problem Statement

Admins currently have no safe way to retire a Publisher, Game, Team, Character, Product, SKU, or Account. The only available operation is a hard delete, which immediately destroys the row and breaks any existing Order history that references it. Admins need to remove items from the live storefront without corrupting historical data.

## Solution

Introduce a `deletedAt` timestamp field on the seven affected entities. A non-null `deletedAt` means the entity is retired: it disappears from all public and admin list/detail endpoints, and cascades retire its dependants. Orders and Carts are unaffected. Accounts with a non-null `deletedAt` are locked out of the API immediately.

## User Stories

1. As an Admin, I want to delete a Publisher so that it no longer appears in the catalog or storefront.
2. As an Admin, I want to delete a Game so that it no longer appears in the catalog or storefront.
3. As an Admin, I want to delete a Team so that it no longer appears in the catalog or storefront.
4. As an Admin, I want to delete a Character so that it no longer appears in the catalog or storefront.
5. As an Admin, I want to delete a Product so that Buyers can no longer view or purchase it.
6. As an Admin, I want to delete a SKU so that Buyers can no longer add it to their Cart or purchase it.
7. As an Admin, I want to delete a Publisher and have all of its Games automatically retired, so that I do not have to cascade the action manually.
8. As an Admin, I want to delete a Game and have all of its Teams, Characters, and associated Products and SKUs automatically retired, so that the entire sub-catalog is consistently hidden.
9. As an Admin, I want to delete a Team and have all Products tagged with that Team automatically retired, so that orphaned Products do not remain visible.
10. As an Admin, I want to delete a Character and have all Products tagged with that Character automatically retired, so that orphaned Products do not remain visible.
11. As an Admin, I want to delete a Product and have all of its SKUs automatically retired, so that the variants cannot be purchased independently.
12. As a Buyer, I want to receive a 404 when I navigate to a deleted Product URL, so that I understand the item is no longer available rather than seeing a broken page.
13. As a Buyer, I want to receive a 404 when I navigate to a deleted Game, Team, Character, or Publisher page, so that the storefront remains consistent.
14. As a Buyer, I want to be prevented from adding a deleted SKU to my Cart, so that I cannot initiate a purchase for a retired item.
15. As an Admin, I want deleted entities to be excluded from all list responses, so that the catalog management UI reflects only live items.
16. As an Admin, I want to soft-delete an Account so that the Account holder is locked out of the API immediately.
17. As a locked-out Account holder, I want to receive a 401 on any authenticated request after my Account is deleted, so that I know my access has been revoked.
18. As an Admin, I want Orders placed by a deleted Account to remain intact and accessible, so that fulfillment and audit history are not disrupted.
19. As an Admin, I want Order history that references a deleted SKU or Product to remain intact, so that financial records are preserved even after catalog retirement.
20. As a system operator, I want Cart and CartItem records to be unaffected by soft delete, so that in-flight guest sessions are not disrupted.
21. As an Admin, I want no delete endpoint to exist on Orders or OrderItems, so that financial records are immutable by design.

## Implementation Decisions

- **Schema**: Add `deletedAt DateTime?` (nullable timestamp, null = active) to `Publisher`, `Game`, `Team`, `Character`, `Product`, `Sku`, and `Account`. No schema changes to `Cart`, `CartItem`, `Order`, `OrderItem`.
- **BaseRepository**: Add a `softRemove(id)` method that sets `deletedAt = now()`. Keep the existing `remove(id)` hard-delete for Cart/CartItem usage. All soft-delete entity repositories call `softRemove`; their service `remove` methods are updated accordingly.
- **Per-repository filtering**: Every `findById`, `findAll`, `findBySlug`, and similar query on the seven affected repositories adds an explicit `where: { deletedAt: null }` condition. No global Prisma middleware or client extension. This makes the filter grep-able and keeps admin/audit queries straightforward.
- **Cascade on soft-delete**: When a parent entity is soft-deleted, its dependants are cascade soft-deleted in the same request within the service layer (not at the DB level). Cascade hierarchy: Publisher → Games → Teams, Characters, Products → SKUs; Game → Teams, Characters, Products (by gameId) → SKUs; Team → Products (by teamId) → SKUs; Character → Products (by characterId) → SKUs; Product → SKUs. A Product with multiple Facets (e.g. both a Team and a Game) is retired if any one of its Facet parents is retired.
- **API response for deleted resources**: 404 Not Found. Soft-deleted entities are indistinguishable from never-existing ones from the caller's perspective.
- **No recovery endpoint**: Soft delete is write-only. There is no restore/un-delete API. Manual DB intervention via Prisma Studio is the recovery path.
- **Account lockout**: The auth guard (ClerkGuard) fetches the Account row after verifying the Clerk token. If `deletedAt` is non-null, it throws `UnauthorizedException` (401). No Clerk-side action is taken — Clerk account management remains a separate Admin concern.
- **Order/OrderItem immutability**: No delete endpoints are introduced for `Order` or `OrderItem`. Existing FK constraints (`ON DELETE SET NULL` for `Order.accountId`, `ON DELETE RESTRICT` for `OrderItem.skuId`) are unchanged. Soft-deleted SKUs retain their DB rows, so Order history FK references remain valid.
- **Publishers, Games, Teams**: Per ADR context, these are seeded, not runtime-managed by Admins. Delete endpoints for these entities are added as admin-only operations alongside the soft-delete implementation to enable retirement without a re-seed.

## Testing Decisions

- **What makes a good test**: Tests assert observable HTTP behavior against a real database — status codes, response bodies, and absence of data in list responses. No mocking of repositories or services. Tests must not assert on internal fields like `deletedAt` directly from the API response (since deleted entities return 404, not a response body).
- **Seam**: Single seam — HTTP integration tests via supertest against the full `AppModule` with a real PrismaService. Same pattern as `test/integration/catalog.integration.spec.ts` and `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts`.
- **Coverage per entity**: For each of the seven entities: (1) seed entity → DELETE → subsequent GET returns 404; (2) seed entity → DELETE → entity absent from list response.
- **Cascade coverage**: Seed parent + children → DELETE parent → GET on each child class returns 404.
- **Account lockout**: Seed Account → DELETE Account → authenticated request returns 401.
- **Order history preservation**: Seed SKU + Order referencing it → DELETE SKU → Order detail endpoint still returns the OrderItem with the SKU reference intact.
- **Prior art**: `test/integration/catalog.integration.spec.ts` (seed/teardown pattern), `test/integration/admin.integration.spec.ts` (Admin auth pattern).

## Out of Scope

- Recovery/restore endpoint for soft-deleted entities.
- Clerk-side account deletion or banning when an Account is soft-deleted.
- GDPR data anonymization beyond blocking API access.
- Soft delete on `Cart`, `CartItem`, `Order`, or `OrderItem`.
- Hard-delete removal of existing `.remove()` callers on Cart (those remain unchanged).
- Admin UI for viewing or filtering deleted entities.

## Further Notes

- FK constraints remain unchanged. Soft delete does not remove rows, so `ON DELETE RESTRICT` constraints on SKU and other entities are never triggered by the soft-delete path.
- The `Sku.available` boolean (Availability flag) is independent of `deletedAt`. A soft-deleted SKU is filtered out of queries before availability is considered, so no synchronization between the two fields is needed.
- All cascade logic lives in the service layer, not in DB triggers or Prisma middleware, consistent with the project's modular monolith pattern.
close_reason: All 7 sub-tasks closed (foundation, Product/SKU, Game, Team, Character, Account, Publisher); order-history preservation covered by soft-delete-product-sku tests

title: Implement POST /cart/sync to merge localStorage cart on sign-in
description: After sign-in, the frontend has items in localStorage that need merging into the server-side cart session. Need a POST /cart/sync endpoint that accepts an array of {skuId, quantity} line items and upserts them into the authenticated user's cart, respecting existing quantities (add, not overwrite).

title: Introduce CATALOG_READ_PORT to decouple Payments from Catalog concrete class
description: PaymentsService injects CatalogReadService (concrete class) directly across module boundary. In our Modular Monolith arch, cross-module deps must go through port tokens (same pattern as SUPPLIER_PORT and NOTIFICATION_PORT). Fix: create catalog-read.port.ts with interface + token, implement on CatalogReadService, export token from CatalogModule, update PaymentsService and its spec to inject by token.

title: Fix orphaned no-facet product leaking into test DB
description: The facet filter test seeds a product with no gameId ('No-facet product') to verify it's excluded from facet queries. cleanupCatalog() walks publisher→games→products, so this orphan is invisible to cleanup and leaks into the test DB permanently. Fix: capture p3.id and delete it explicitly in afterAll.

title: Refactor: deepen Order intake into OrdersService
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

PaymentsService.confirmOrder (68 lines) spans four domain concerns — cart read, order creation, fulfillment dispatch, notification — with zero locality. OrdersService has no create method; order intake is untestable without Stripe.

Add `OrdersService.confirm(intent: ConfirmedPaymentIntent)` that contains all logic currently in PaymentsService.confirmOrder: idempotency check, cart read, Order creation (via Prisma), CartService.clearCart(), supplier dispatch via SUPPLIER_PORT, notification via NOTIFICATION_PORT, and order status update. PaymentsService.handleWebhook calls `orders.confirm()` and knows nothing else about order creation.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] OrdersService.confirm() is the single place where Orders are created
- [ ] PaymentsService.confirmOrder is deleted; replaced by a single orders.confirm() call
- [ ] CartService.clearCart() is called from OrdersService (not bypassed via direct Prisma)
- [ ] OrdersService injects NOTIFICATION_PORT, not the concrete NotificationsService
- [ ] Integration test: given a ConfirmedPaymentIntent, confirm() creates Order, clears Cart, calls supplier and notification ports — no Stripe client required
- [ ] Existing integration tests pass

## Blocked by

- merch-shop-BE-j0g — Account provisioning: upsert Account on first auth
- merch-shop-BE-bd2 — Prefactor: introduce NOTIFICATION_PORT seam

title: Prefactor: export a read interface from CatalogModule
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

CatalogModule currently exports nothing. Other modules (Payments computing cart total, Commerce checking SKU availability) bypass CatalogModule and hit Prisma directly. No seam exists for catalog reads — schema changes in the catalog ripple freely into other modules.

Export a small read facade from CatalogModule: `findProducts(filter)`, `getSkuPrice(skuId)`, `findPublisher(slug)`. The 6 internal services remain as implementation details. PaymentsService and CommerceModule use the exported interface; they declare `CatalogModule` in their `imports` array.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] CatalogModule exports a read interface (facade or service) with `findProducts`, `getSkuPrice`, `findPublisher`
- [ ] PaymentsService.createPaymentIntent uses `CatalogModule.getSkuPrice` not raw Prisma for SKU price
- [ ] CommerceModule declares `CatalogModule` in its `imports`
- [ ] Integration tests pass

## Blocked by

merch-shop-BE-772 — Prefactor: surface PrismaModule coupling in module declarations

title: Prefactor: introduce NOTIFICATION_PORT seam
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

PaymentsService imports NotificationsService as a concrete class. No seam — the Resend adapter is welded to the payment path and integration tests require a live Resend connection.

Mirror the existing `SUPPLIER_PORT` pattern. Create a `NOTIFICATION_PORT` injection token and `NotificationPort` interface with a single `sendOrderConfirmation(data)` method. `ResendAdapter` implements it in prod. A `LogNotificationAdapter` (no-op) is registered for integration tests. PaymentsService injects `NOTIFICATION_PORT` via `@Inject(NOTIFICATION_PORT)` — no longer importing the concrete class.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `NOTIFICATION_PORT` symbol and `NotificationPort` interface exist in the notifications module
- [ ] `ResendAdapter` is the prod implementation wired in NotificationsModule
- [ ] `LogNotificationAdapter` registered for integration test context
- [ ] PaymentsService injects `NOTIFICATION_PORT`, not the concrete `NotificationsService`
- [ ] Integration tests run without a Resend API key

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

title: Prefactor: extract cart identity resolution to CartSessionContext
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

`CartController.resolveSession()` — deciding whether a request is guest or authenticated — is domain logic duplicated across 3 controller methods inside the HTTP layer. Extract it.

Create a NestJS param decorator `@CartSession()` that returns a `CartSessionContext` value object `{ type: 'guest' | 'account', id: string }`. Remove the `resolveSession()` private method from CartController. All three handler methods (get, addItem, removeItem) receive `CartSessionContext` as a typed parameter. Update `CartService.getOrCreate` to accept it.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `@CartSession()` decorator extracts identity from request (cookie `sessionId` or Clerk JWT `accountId`)
- [ ] CartController has no `resolveSession()` method
- [ ] CartService.getOrCreate accepts CartSessionContext
- [ ] Integration tests for guest cart and account cart pass

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.

title: Prefactor: surface PrismaModule coupling in module declarations
description:
## Parent
merch-shop-BE-13e

## What to build

Remove `@Global()` from PrismaModule so that each module's DB dependency is visible in its own `imports` array. Currently 10 service files inject PrismaService but zero modules declare it — the module graph is misleading.

Add `PrismaModule` to `imports` in CatalogModule, CommerceModule, PaymentsModule, and AuthModule. No logic changes — purely structural.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] PrismaModule has no `@Global()` decorator
- [ ] CatalogModule, CommerceModule, PaymentsModule, AuthModule each declare PrismaModule in imports
- [ ] App compiles without errors
- [ ] Integration tests pass

## Blocked by

None — can start immediately.
